# Deliberate Creation

**Flexibility: Expected (SHOULD)**

## The Commitment

Creating a persistent entity — an agent, a venture, a governance object, a team —
SHOULD require structured deliberation. Default values are presented as suggestions,
not silently inherited. Every parameter is consciously chosen or explicitly accepted.

## What This Means

- Creation processes present options and ask for decisions, not just apply defaults.
- "Inheritance" from templates is transparent — the creator sees what they're inheriting.
- Parameters left unaddressed are flagged, not silently defaulted.
- The creation event is logged with full context: who, why, what was decided.

## Violation Looks Like

A new agent is created by copying an existing one with no review of whether
the copied parameters are appropriate. A new venture inherits governance
settings from a template without the creator knowing what was inherited.

## Derived From

- JOS-C04 (Configuration Density) — configuration is set deliberately
- JOS-C15 (The Agent Line) — crossing the agent line requires deliberate creation

### Framework Connection

Framework §7 (Empty/Null/Filled three-state model) operationalizes deliberate creation — an Empty slot is an explicit invitation that must be consciously filled, while a Null slot is a deliberate decision not to fill, preventing silent inheritance.
